From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Series of post-apocalyptic novels by S. M. Stirling

The Emberverse series

**The Emberverse series**—or **Change World**[[1]](#cite_note-1)—is a series of [post-apocalyptic](/wiki/Post-apocalyptic "Post-apocalyptic") [alternate history](/wiki/Alternate_history "Alternate history") novels written by [S. M. Stirling](/wiki/S._M._Stirling "S. M. Stirling").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

The novels depict the events following a mysterious—yet sudden—worldwide event called "The Change" that occurs at 6:15 p.m. [Pacific Standard Time](/wiki/Pacific_Time_Zone "Pacific Time Zone"), March 17, 1998. The Change alters both the course of history and all physical laws when it causes all the [electricity](/wiki/Electricity "Electricity"), [firearms](/wiki/Gunpowder "Gunpowder"), [explosives](/wiki/Explosive_material "Explosive material"), [internal combustion engines](/wiki/Internal_combustion_engine "Internal combustion engine"), [steam power](/wiki/Steam_power "Steam power") and most forms of high-energy-density technology on Earth to permanently no longer work. Most of the action in the series takes place in the [Willamette Valley](/wiki/Willamette_Valley "Willamette Valley") of [Oregon](/wiki/Oregon "Oregon") in the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States"). The series primarily focuses on how the characters survive the loss of 600 years of technological progress. The first book, *Dies the Fire*, concerns the conflicts between a [Portland](/wiki/Portland,_Oregon "Portland, Oregon")-based neo-[feudalist](/wiki/Feudalism "Feudalism") dictatorship and the free communities of the Willamette Valley, primarily the Bearkillers and the [Wiccan](/wiki/Wicca "Wicca") Clan Mackenzie. The later series, *The Change*, focuses on the now-adult children of the original trilogy's major characters. A third sub-series, beginning with *The Golden Princess*, features the grandchildren of the original survivors as the central characters, and concluded with 2018's *The Sky-Blue Wolves*. The Emberverse is closely related to the preceding Stirling [Nantucket series](/wiki/Nantucket_series "Nantucket series"). Both deal with the aftermath of The Change, though its effect is radically different in the two series.

### Short stories

## Post-Change Oregon

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Emberverse_series&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Post-Change Oregon")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c1/DtFmap960.gif/250px-DtFmap960.gif)](/wiki/File:DtFmap960.gif)

A map of Post-Change Oregon

Though the Change caused a large loss of life everywhere, Oregon suffered less than most places of similar population density. Although the region lost over 95 percent of its population, it was spared being turned into a "death zone" with no survivors except bands of cannibals—the usual fate for areas around large cities—for several reasons.

First, much of the population of Portland—the largest city in the region—was forced out by the Portland Protective Association (PPA) and either died *en masse* or joined the tens of thousands congregated in large refugee camps around [Salem](/wiki/Salem,_Oregon "Salem, Oregon"). Second, the Oregon state government prevented refugees from foraging in the rural areas without government approval. This protected the rural areas from losing their food and supplies, especially since rural citizens tended to fight off the foragers successfully. Third, large numbers of people living in refugee camps with limited medical supplies became susceptible to disease, including the [bubonic plague](/wiki/Bubonic_plague "Bubonic plague"). Thanks to strict quarantine methods, the rural communities in Oregon were spared the worst effects of the plague.[[16]](#cite_note-16)

Post-Change Oregon history is then marked with the PPA's attempts to conquer the entire Willamette Valley, but the southern communities—led by the Bearkillers and Clan Mackenzie—successfully opposed them.[[17]](#cite_note-17)[[18]](#cite_note-18) The last attempt was called the War of the Eye, or the Protector's War, and ended with the mutual deaths of Lord Protector Arminger at the hands of Lord Bear Havel, who died shortly thereafter from his own wounds from the duel. The end of the war saw the end of the PPA's attempts at southern conquest, and the creation of "The Meeting", a United Nations–like organization dedicated to keeping the peace among the different factions in the valley.[[19]](#cite_note-19) A decade later, the nations of the Meeting became embroiled in a war with the combined forces of the [Church Universal and Triumphant](/wiki/Church_Universal_and_Triumphant "Church Universal and Triumphant") and the United States of [Boise](/wiki/Boise "Boise").

### Portland Protective Association (PPA)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Emberverse_series&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Portland Protective Association (PPA)")]

The **Portland Protective Association** is a neo-[feudal](/wiki/Feudal "Feudal") [absolute monarchy](/wiki/Absolute_monarchy "Absolute monarchy") founded by history professor and [Society for Creative Anachronism](/wiki/Society_for_Creative_Anachronism "Society for Creative Anachronism") (SCA) member Norman Arminger and his wife Sandra, aided by former [urban gang](/wiki/Gang "Gang") members and members of the SCA. The nation follows medieval [Norman](/wiki/Normans "Normans") customs and practices. The nation builds castles to protect its farming communities and serve as the centers of regional government. The PPA aristocracy often refers to its members as *Associates*. For a time, the PPA had an [antipope](/wiki/Antipope "Antipope"), Leo XIV, and operated an [Inquisition](/wiki/Inquisition "Inquisition"), but this ended after contact was re-established with [the real Pope](/wiki/Pope "Pope") in Badia, Italy.

[Knights](/wiki/Knight "Knight") are the strength of the PPA military, and are trained from childhood to fight as armored horsemen, often on barded horses. Since the death of Norman Arminger at the end of the War of the Eye, Sandra rules for her daughter Mathilda as a [regent](/wiki/Regent "Regent"). The national [heraldic](/wiki/Heraldry "Heraldry") symbol adopted by the PPA is the *Lidless Eye*, an allusion to the [Eye of Sauron](/wiki/Sauron#Eye_of_Sauron "Sauron") from [J. R. R. Tolkien](/wiki/J._R._R._Tolkien "J. R. R. Tolkien")'s *[The Lord of the Rings](/wiki/The_Lord_of_the_Rings "The Lord of the Rings")*.[[20]](#cite_note-link-20)

The PPA is ruled from Portland, and stretches from [Astoria](/wiki/Astoria,_Oregon "Astoria, Oregon") to [Pendleton](/wiki/Pendleton,_Oregon "Pendleton, Oregon"). The Protector's Palace was once the Portland [Central Library](/wiki/Central_Library_(Portland,_Oregon) "Central Library (Portland, Oregon)").

### Bearkillers

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Emberverse_series&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Bearkillers")]

A military organization founded by former [Marine](/wiki/United_States_Marine_Corps "United States Marine Corps") Mike Havel, the **Bearkillers** is composed of refugees from the Change. The group emigrated from its beginnings in [Idaho](/wiki/Idaho "Idaho") to a base at Larsdalen (the former Larsson family ranch) in Oregon. Havel saved the wealthy Larsson family from death after crash-landing their light airplane in the mountains of Idaho; they later formed the nucleus of the Bearkillers.

The lands held by the Bearkillers are divided into [thorps](/wiki/Thorp "Thorp"), outlying farms centering upon a fortified settlement with a [smithy](/wiki/Blacksmith "Blacksmith"), mill, and other utilitarian buildings. These thorps are usually the estates of "A Listers", on which live an "A Lister" family and those families and individuals who are under their protection.

The Bearkillers military élite are the **A Listers**, who become part of the brotherhood of the Bearkillers after completing an apprenticeship program. Membership in the A List is open to men and women who are able to meet the rigorous physical requirements and have passed the requisite training regimen. At their graduation, new "A Listers" are sworn onto the rolls, and branded between the eyebrows with a small vertical line. The Bearkillers' military is noted for its use of [pikes](/wiki/Pike_(weapon) "Pike (weapon)") and [horse archers](/wiki/Horse_archer "Horse archer"), but are most feared for their skill with the [backsword](/wiki/Backsword "Backsword").

The Bearkillers are governed by a leader, titled *Lord Bear*, and a democratic council. The heraldic symbol of the Bearkillers is a red or crimson snarling bear's head on brown.[[20]](#cite_note-link-20)

Bearkillers celebrate the day of the Change (March 17) as a holiday known as *[Gunpowder](/wiki/Gunpowder "Gunpowder") Day*. Festivities include the Lord Bear setting a bowl of gunpowder on fire to test whether the laws of physics have been restored to their original condition, a feast with representatives from local nations attending, and the induction of new members into the Brotherhood of the Bearkillers.

The Bearkillers do not have a uniform religion, with some members venerating the Norse [Æsir](/wiki/%C3%86sir "Æsir"), some [Wiccan](/wiki/Wicca "Wicca"), and others worshipping the Christian God in various ways.

### Clan Mackenzie

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Emberverse_series&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Clan Mackenzie")]

**Clan Mackenzie** is a [Georgian Wiccan](/wiki/Georgian_Wicca "Georgian Wicca") [clan](/wiki/Clan "Clan") founded by folk-singer and High Priestess Juniper Mackenzie and members of her Singing Moon [coven](/wiki/Coven "Coven") who fled to her country home.[[21]](#cite_note-dies_fire_8-21)[[22]](#cite_note-22) After being rebuilt into a fortified [homestead](/wiki/Homestead_(buildings) "Homestead (buildings)"), the home was renamed Dun Juniper and became the capital of the faction. The Clan includes several other Duns that were built as the population expanded, as well as the [walled city](/wiki/Walled_city "Walled city") of Sutterdown, which is based on the town of [Brownsville](/wiki/Brownsville,_Oregon "Brownsville, Oregon").

Clan Mackenzie's weapon of choice is the [longbow](/wiki/Longbow "Longbow"), but in hand-to-hand fighting they use the [gladius](/wiki/Gladius "Gladius") and buckler.

The Clan practices religious freedom, but there are few non-Wiccans in the population, mostly due to mass conversions after the Change. The heraldic symbol of the clan is a [crescent moon](/wiki/Crescent "Crescent") above silver [elk](/wiki/Elk "Elk") horns on green. The Mackenzie [tartan](/wiki/Tartan "Tartan") is colored green, brown, and black, with thin orange piping.[[20]](#cite_note-link-20) This is not the color of the historical [Clan Mackenzie](/wiki/Clan_Mackenzie "Clan Mackenzie"), whose tartan is white, red, green, and blue.[[23]](#cite_note-23)

### Mount Angel

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Emberverse_series&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Mount Angel")]

After the Change, the [Benedictine](/wiki/Benedictine "Benedictine") monks of **[Mount Angel Abbey](/wiki/Mount_Angel_Abbey "Mount Angel Abbey")** aided the local town and refugees through the worst of the crisis. Similar to organizations like the [Knights Templar](/wiki/Knights_Templar "Knights Templar"), the monks and sisters of the Abbey now maintain martial skills to protect their nation. Mount Angel Abbey itself was rebuilt into a large fortress that is considered the strongest in the area. The head of the Abbey is also the most influential leader of [Mount Angel](/wiki/Mt._Angel,_Oregon "Mt. Angel, Oregon"), though the town elects a mayor to govern it in secular matters. The Mount Angel banner carries the image of [Virgin](/wiki/Blessed_Virgin_Mary_(Roman_Catholic) "Blessed Virgin Mary (Roman Catholic)") and [Child](/wiki/Child_Jesus "Child Jesus").[[20]](#cite_note-link-20)

### Corvallis

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Emberverse_series&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Corvallis")]

**[Corvallis](/wiki/Corvallis,_Oregon "Corvallis, Oregon")** is a [city-state](/wiki/City-state "City-state") at the southern end of the Willamette Valley. The former faculty of [Oregon State University](/wiki/Oregon_State_University "Oregon State University") governs the city as the Faculty Senate, which holds public assemblies in the [Gill Coliseum](/wiki/Gill_Coliseum "Gill Coliseum"). The traditions of the university have been adopted by the new nation, with [the school fight song](/wiki/Hail_to_Old_OSU "Hail to Old OSU") acting as the national anthem and the school mascot as their flag.[[20]](#cite_note-link-20) It is a very wealthy state that also controls the port town of [Newport](/wiki/Newport,_Oregon "Newport, Oregon"). It serves as the headquarters of The Meeting.

The Corvallis military prefers jointed pikes as their weapon of choice, bolstered by catapults, ballistae, and other siege engines.

### Dúnedain Rangers

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Emberverse_series&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Dúnedain Rangers")]

Founded by Astrid Larsson (of the Bearkiller-Larsson family) and Eilir Mackenzie, the **[Dúnedain](/wiki/D%C3%BAnedain "Dúnedain") Rangers** are a semi-[mercenary](/wiki/Mercenary "Mercenary") military organization that protects caravans and fights brigands in the Willamette Valley. The Ranger lifestyle is based largely on *The Lord of the Rings* by J.R.R. Tolkien—which they refer to as "the Histories"—even to the point of requiring all members to learn [the Elvish language](/wiki/Sindarin "Sindarin"). Rangers are also required to learn [sign language](/wiki/Sign_language "Sign language") as part of their training. The Rangers operate out of Mithrilwood, which is located in the old [Silver Falls State Park](/wiki/Silver_Falls_State_Park "Silver Falls State Park"), centered upon their settlement, Stardell Hall. The Rangers' flag features a silver tree and seven stars, similar to the [royal standard of Gondor](/wiki/Gondor#Heraldry_and_heirlooms "Gondor") as depicted in *The Lord of the Rings*.[[20]](#cite_note-link-20)

### Central Oregon Ranchers Association (CORA)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Emberverse_series&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Central Oregon Ranchers Association (CORA)")]

The **Central Oregon Ranchers Association** is a [confederation](/wiki/Confederation "Confederation") of independent ranchers and farmers located in [Central Oregon](/wiki/Central_Oregon "Central Oregon"). After the Change, the ranchers and farmers of the area took in refugees who now work in exchange for settlement land. CORA's warriors prefer to ride horseback and wield [bows](/wiki/Composite_bow "Composite bow"), [sabres](/wiki/Sabre "Sabre"), and [javelins](/wiki/Javelin "Javelin"). The capital is located at [Bend](/wiki/Bend,_Oregon "Bend, Oregon").

### Confederated Tribes of Warm Springs

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Emberverse_series&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Confederated Tribes of Warm Springs")]

The **[Confederated Tribes of Warm Springs](/wiki/Confederated_Tribes_of_Warm_Springs "Confederated Tribes of Warm Springs")** are a confederation of [Native American](/wiki/Indigenous_peoples_of_the_Americas "Indigenous peoples of the Americas") tribes, originally confined to the [Warm Springs Indian Reservation](/wiki/Warm_Springs_Indian_Reservation "Warm Springs Indian Reservation"), which expanded after the Change. Warm Springs accepted refugees from the Change to settle in their territory and help with farming. Many of these refugees adopted the local customs and traditions of the tribes. They remained neutral during the War of the Eye, but did allow CORA forces to pass through their territory.

### Pendleton

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Emberverse_series&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Pendleton")]

Shortly after the Change, the town of **[Pendleton](/wiki/Pendleton,_Oregon "Pendleton, Oregon")** and the local ranchers became embroiled in a civil war against a nearby Native American reservation. For a short time the area was a duchy of the PPA, but the PPA was forced to withdraw after the end of the War of the Eye. By 2020, there was a nominal government located at Pendleton, but the local ranchers continue to exercise influence during the annual [Pendleton Round-Up](/wiki/Pendleton_Round-Up "Pendleton Round-Up"). The area is lawless and [slavery](/wiki/Slavery "Slavery") is still practiced.

## Post-Change North America

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Emberverse_series&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Post-Change North America")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/e/e7/StirlingMapFinalC.jpg/250px-StirlingMapFinalC.jpg)](/wiki/File:StirlingMapFinalC.jpg)

Map of the Post-Change United States

After the Change, millions of [North Americans](/wiki/North_American "North American") were denied the technology upon which they had been dependent. The [Eastern United States](/wiki/Eastern_United_States "Eastern United States"), most of [Texas](/wiki/Texas "Texas") and [California](/wiki/California "California") were some of the worst hit, as starving urbanites ate the remaining food in the area before resorting to [cannibalism](/wiki/Human_cannibalism "Human cannibalism"). Pockets of civilization, mostly around the [Great Plains](/wiki/Great_Plains "Great Plains"), survived where there were no large cities around. Several new nations sprang up to keep order in the changed world.[[24]](#cite_note-sunrise-lands-24)

The new governments were organized along a [feudal](/wiki/Feudalism "Feudalism") structure, with American terms adapting to describe medieval concepts. Memory of the United States continues to affect the culture of these nations. Many of these nations continue to fly the [US flag](/wiki/US_flag "US flag"), and several leaders take the term "[President of the United States](/wiki/President_of_the_United_States "President of the United States")" along with their other titles.

The [Sioux](/wiki/Sioux "Sioux") and [Haida](/wiki/Haida_people "Haida people") also carved out sizable nations in North America. The Haida returned to the practice of slavery, raiding the Oregon coast for new slaves. The Sioux live a nomadic lifestyle, taking care of their herds and hunting [buffalo](/wiki/American_bison "American bison"), though also raiding their neighbors unless they are paid protection money. The Sioux made an unsuccessful attempt to conquer Fargo and Marshall, and fought a war with the Church Universal and Triumphant that led to a peace treaty seen as somewhat unfavorable to the Sioux.

In [Hawaiʻi](/wiki/Hawaii "Hawaii"), the population of [Oʻahu](/wiki/Oahu "Oahu") wiped itself out, but civilization still exists on [the Big Island of Hawaiʻi](/wiki/Hawaii_(island) "Hawaii (island)"). The island produces coffee, a rare commodity in post-Change North America. [Prince Edward Island](/wiki/Prince_Edward_Island "Prince Edward Island") also survived relatively intact, becoming a part of the [British Empire](/wiki/British_Empire "British Empire"), compared to most of the heavily populated sections of eastern [Canada](/wiki/Canada "Canada") along the [Saint Lawrence Seaway](/wiki/Saint_Lawrence_Seaway "Saint Lawrence Seaway"), such as [Toronto](/wiki/Toronto "Toronto") and [Montreal](/wiki/Montreal "Montreal"), which also became a Death Zone. [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico") and [Central America](/wiki/Central_America "Central America"), including the [Caribbean](/wiki/Caribbean "Caribbean"), were almost completely depopulated by overlapping Death Zones, though by 2050, [Jamaica](/wiki/Jamaica "Jamaica") would recover enough to have merchants trade in British ports. [South America](/wiki/South_America "South America") adapted to post-Change life much as North America did, with rural areas far from large cities maintaining much of their population and adapting to the new laws of nature. [Chocolate](/wiki/Chocolate "Chocolate") was imported from the [Caribbean Sea](/wiki/Caribbean_Sea "Caribbean Sea"), but only after trade routes were re-established by settler populations from the south and from the British Empire. By 2050, Britain laid claim to the east coast of North America.[[25]](#cite_note-SC-25)

### Church Universal and Triumphant

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Emberverse_series&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Church Universal and Triumphant")]

Located in [Paradise Valley, Montana](/wiki/Paradise_Valley_(Montana) "Paradise Valley (Montana)"), the **[Church Universal and Triumphant](/wiki/Church_Universal_and_Triumphant "Church Universal and Triumphant")** (CUT) built a [theocratic](/wiki/Theocracy "Theocracy") empire controlling most of [Montana](/wiki/Montana "Montana") and [Wyoming](/wiki/Wyoming "Wyoming"). Under the leadership of a mysterious madman, known as the Prophet, the CUT aggressively spread their religion, often resorting to conquest to do so. The Prophet has since died, leaving his son Sethaz to continue as Prophet. Soldiers of CUT are commonly called "Cutters".

### United States of America (Boise)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Emberverse_series&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: United States of America (Boise)")]

Founded by President Thurston in [Boise](/wiki/Boise,_Idaho "Boise, Idaho"), the **United States of America** claimed all of the territory of the old United States, vowing to once again reunite the country. Although they called themselves the United States of America, they were commonly referred to by their neighbors as the "**United States of Boise**". The nation follows the [military practices](/wiki/Military_of_ancient_Rome "Military of ancient Rome") of the [Roman Empire](/wiki/Roman_Empire "Roman Empire"). It is nominally a democracy, though elections have not been held; President Thurston promised to hold elections after a battle with the CUT. He was purposely killed late during said battle by his eldest son, Martin Thurston, who assumed leadership of the country.

### New Deseret

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Emberverse_series&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: New Deseret")]

A [Mormon](/wiki/Mormon "Mormon") [theocracy](/wiki/Theocracy "Theocracy"), **New [Deseret](/wiki/State_of_Deseret "State of Deseret")** covers northern [Utah](/wiki/Utah "Utah") and southern [Idaho](/wiki/Idaho "Idaho"). At the beginning of the *Change* series, the nation was in a losing war with the CUT. By the end of *The Scourge of God*, they were occupied by the CUT, although bands of rebels continued to offer resistance.

### Provisional Republic of Iowa

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Emberverse_series&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Provisional Republic of Iowa")]

[Iowa](/wiki/Iowa "Iowa") managed to weather the Change, thanks to its rural economy, low population, and the fact that the Governor closed the bridges across the [Mississippi River](/wiki/Mississippi_River "Mississippi River") so starving refugees from the eastern states could not enter. By the *Change* tetralogy, the **Provisional Republic of Iowa** was one of the largest and wealthiest nations in North America. Farmers acted as [landed gentry](/wiki/Landed_gentry "Landed gentry") in Iowa society, with city evacuees serving as [serfs](/wiki/Serf "Serf"). The position of Governor is hereditary, despite the nation's nominal status as a republic. Iowa also established a small presence in the former territories of [Illinois](/wiki/Illinois "Illinois") and [Nebraska](/wiki/Nebraska "Nebraska"). The nation aided Fargo and Marshall in their war with the Sioux.

### Norrheim

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Emberverse_series&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Norrheim")]

Most of the [New England](/wiki/New_England "New England") area did not survive the Change. However a few small towns in Northern [Maine](/wiki/Maine "Maine") did and like the Clan Mackenzie came under the influence of one person's spiritual views. Using *[Asatru](/wiki/Asatru "Asatru")* this area became home to a group that began to think of themselves as [Vikings](/wiki/Vikings "Vikings").

## Post-Change world

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Emberverse_series&action=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Post-Change world")]

Due to the difficulties in communicating across the world caused by the Change, information about the world outside North America is scant.

In [Great Britain](/wiki/Great_Britain "Great Britain"), the [royal family](/wiki/Royal_family "Royal family") and others were evacuated to the [Isle of Wight](/wiki/Isle_of_Wight "Isle of Wight") on the third day after The Change to protect them from the starving rioters in [London](/wiki/London "London") and other major cities. [Elizabeth II](/wiki/Elizabeth_II "Elizabeth II") died early during the events of the Change (in December 1998), and was succeeded by her eldest son [Prince Charles](/wiki/Charles_III "Charles III") as Charles III and later in his reign is known as "Mad King Charles". After Charles III died from a stroke in 2008, his elder son [Prince William](/wiki/William,_Prince_of_Wales "William, Prince of Wales") became the king as William V. Resettlement later began from Wight and other islands, and farmland was reestablished in England and continental Europe. Contact was made with other parts of the world, including [Iceland](/wiki/Iceland "Iceland"), which removed a substantial portion of its population to Britain in order to survive. [Gibraltar](/wiki/Gibraltar "Gibraltar"), [Ulster](/wiki/Ulster "Ulster"), and [Prince Edward Island](/wiki/Prince_Edward_Island "Prince Edward Island") all survived and swore loyalty to the British government; along with the new British colonies in Europe and North Africa, this led to a rebirth of the [British Empire](/wiki/British_Empire "British Empire").

Other parts of Europe also survived the Change and prospered. Surviving parts of [Scandinavia](/wiki/Scandinavia "Scandinavia") formed the nation of Norrland and divided Western Europe with Britain along the old German border. Parts of [Ireland](/wiki/Ireland "Ireland") besides Ulster survived, including the Republic of the [Shannon](/wiki/Shannon,_County_Clare "Shannon, County Clare") and, in the north, the [Republic of Ireland](/wiki/Republic_of_Ireland "Republic of Ireland") (Provisional), called "Provoland"—a fanatical Republican entity. In [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy"), three nations arose out of the Change: Venice, [Sicily](/wiki/Sicily "Sicily") and the [Umbrian](/wiki/Umbria "Umbria") League. Not much is known of Eastern Europe, though it has been reported it had a better survival rate than Western Europe. Comments by the author suggest that Greek survivors from [Cyprus](/wiki/Cyprus "Cyprus") become a power in the region, repopulating the fertile [Egyptian Delta](/wiki/Nile_Delta "Nile Delta") and the Levantine Coasts.[*[attribution needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Attribution_needed "Wikipedia:Attribution needed")*] [Russia](/wiki/Russia "Russia") apparently regressed into a handful of rural kingdoms, including one based around [Belograd](/wiki/Belograd "Belograd"), with the [Cossacks](/wiki/Cossacks "Cossacks") and [Tatars](/wiki/Tatars "Tatars") thriving.

[Pope John Paul II](/wiki/Pope_John_Paul_II "Pope John Paul II") died in the [Vatican](/wiki/Holy_See "Holy See") at the time of the Change, instead of surviving until 2005, as in our timeline, but [Cardinal Ratzinger](/wiki/Pope_Benedict_XVI "Pope Benedict XVI") fled to Umbria, where connections with the worldwide [Roman Catholic church](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_church "Roman Catholic church") were reestablished and he was still elected Pope, as in our timeline – and his pontificate lasts until 2022 (by coincidence, the real Ratzinger/Pope Benedict XVI [lived up to that date](/wiki/Death_and_funeral_of_Pope_Benedict_XVI "Death and funeral of Pope Benedict XVI") but had [resigned in 2013 for health reasons](/wiki/Resignation_of_Pope_Benedict_XVI "Resignation of Pope Benedict XVI")). Perhaps the greatest post-Change success of the Roman Catholic Church was reunification with the [Church of England](/wiki/Church_of_England "Church of England"), which led to the crowning of the British monarch (William V) as [Emperor of the West](/wiki/Roman_Emperor "Roman Emperor").

For a time, Europe was threatened by raids from pirates out of the [Canary Islands](/wiki/Canary_Islands "Canary Islands"), supported by the Emir of [Dakar](/wiki/Dakar "Dakar"). A coalition of European nations joined to retake the Canary Islands and destroy their bases on mainland Africa.[[26]](#cite_note-26)

Merchants from Astoria and Newport traded at several ports in Asia, including one named New Singapore. Areas of [China](/wiki/China "China") that survived came under attack by mounted hordes from the Mongolian steppe. [India](/wiki/India "India") devolved to a fervently nationalistic northeastern state called Hinduraj, with smaller populations surviving south in the [Deccan](/wiki/Deccan_Plateau "Deccan Plateau"). Parts of South America apparently survived, given that Cardinals from there participated in the election of the new Pope. Sicily colonized [Libya](/wiki/Libya "Libya"), while Greater Britain established colonies along the coasts of [Algeria](/wiki/Algeria "Algeria"), [Morocco](/wiki/Morocco "Morocco") and [Mauritania](/wiki/Mauritania "Mauritania"). A scattering of African city-states survived along the coast of the continent including [Cape Town](/wiki/Cape_Town "Cape Town").

[Tasmania](/wiki/Tasmania "Tasmania") and the [South Island](/wiki/South_Island "South Island") of [New Zealand](/wiki/New_Zealand "New Zealand") did better than anywhere else in the world, with no die-off, and they were soon sending peaceful explorers to other countries. While mainland [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia") did not do quite as well, its larger land mass/resources meant that after a few decades, it was able to keep up with its more fortunate neighbors, with several new political entities rising from the ashes. The most important nation in Australia was the Kingdom of Capricornia, ruling Northern Australia, with a capital in [Darwin](/wiki/Darwin,_Northern_Territory "Darwin, Northern Territory").

Shortly after the change, a single member of the [Japanese Royal Family](/wiki/Imperial_House_of_Japan "Imperial House of Japan") was evacuated from [Tokyo](/wiki/Tokyo "Tokyo") by a group of soldiers referred to as the Seventy Loyal Men, though only 46 of them would survive the trip to [Sado, Niigata](/wiki/Sado,_Niigata "Sado, Niigata"). The member of the royal family was Reiko's grandmother, who was either [Princess Mako](/wiki/Princess_Mako_of_Akishino "Princess Mako of Akishino") or [Princess Kako](/wiki/Princess_Kako_of_Akishino "Princess Kako of Akishino").

[Supreme Leader of North Korea](/wiki/List_of_leaders_of_North_Korea "List of leaders of North Korea") [Kim Jong-Il](/wiki/Kim_Jong-Il "Kim Jong-Il"), under the influence of the same Powers who guided the Church Universal and Triumphant in the United States, conquered much of mainland Eastern Asia following the Change. Following his death, he would be succeeded by his son, but it has yet to be revealed if his heir is his real-world successor, [Kim Jong-un](/wiki/Kim_Jong-un "Kim Jong-un").

## Connections to the Nantucket series

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Emberverse_series&action=edit&section=22 "Edit section: Connections to the Nantucket series")]

Stirling has confirmed that the Emberverse series are linked to his [Nantucket series](/wiki/Nantucket_series "Nantucket series").[[27]](#cite_note-ph-27)[*[self-published source?](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability#Self-published_sources "Wikipedia:Verifiability")*] Evidence of this can be found throughout the novels:

* The Change occurs at the same time as the Event that propels [Nantucket](/wiki/Nantucket "Nantucket") back in time: 6:15 p.m. [PST](/wiki/Pacific_Time_Zone "Pacific Time Zone") (9:15 pm [EST](/wiki/Eastern_Time_Zone "Eastern Time Zone")).[[28]](#cite_note-28)
* The Mackenzie Dennis Martin's brother John, a [blacksmith](/wiki/Blacksmith "Blacksmith"), is an important secondary character in the Nantucket series.[[29]](#cite_note-29)
* The Larssons bought their Montana property from the family of William Walker, the principal antagonist of the Nantucket series, and Signe dated him briefly.[[30]](#cite_note-30)
* A general in the Church Universal and Triumphant is named Walker, and may be related to William Walker. At the 2009 DragonCon, Stirling confirmed this relationship and said the Walkers were troublemakers in both worlds.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]
* Ingolf has a vision of Nantucket where he sees a picture of Swindapa.[[31]](#cite_note-31)
* Two of the protagonists of the Nantucket series, Marion Alston and Swindapa, appear with Juniper Mackenzie at the end of *The Sword of the Lady* as aspects of the [Maiden–Mother–Crone](/wiki/Triple_Goddess_(Neopaganism) "Triple Goddess (Neopaganism)") trinity during a vision sequence.[[32]](#cite_note-32)

The connection between the Emberverse series and the Nantucket series has led to some confusion by readers. Science-fiction writer [Paul Di Filippo](/wiki/Paul_Di_Filippo "Paul Di Filippo") appeared to miss the connection entirely:

> I'm a little baffled as to why Stirling set this book in 1998. It seems to me that it requires more suspension of disbelief to pretend the world ended in the past when we know it didn't.[[33]](#cite_note-rev-33)

Paul Skevington, writing for SF Crowsnest, also missed the connection:

> Curiously, Stirling has set this novel in 1998 placing us firmly within the realms of alternative history. The reason for the use of this device is not clear, as it doesn't really impact upon the progress of the narrative. Perhaps the author sought after a sense of immediacy garnered by making the events take place in our past rather than our future. Perhaps he sought to avoid the work being viewed as a prediction of things to come – an obvious trap for many speculative works.[[34]](#cite_note-34)

However, Joan Field noted a basic connection:

> The basic premises of the two series are a precise mirror image of one another. In one, modern Americans are transferred to a past world without modern technology and must introduce this technology in order to survive; in the other, modern Americans are suddenly deprived of the same technology and their survival depends on re-learning and re-creating older skills and technologies. (...) In both, the main conflict is between those who try to build up a fairly decent society – though by no means perfect or flawless – and those who see in the crisis a chance for seizing complete, ruthless power.[[35]](#cite_note-35)

## Major themes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Emberverse_series&action=edit&section=23 "Edit section: Major themes")]

### Leader's effect on a group

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Emberverse_series&action=edit&section=24 "Edit section: Leader's effect on a group")]

Stirling explores a leader's impact on an organization. Havel's small group organizes along military lines, brought on by Havel's time in the Marines, as they travel from the site of their plane crash in Idaho to the Larsson homestead in Oregon. The Mackenzie group adopts a Celtic clan structure to farm and fortify Juniper Mackenzie's family homestead, influenced by Wicca and Mackenzie's Celtic heritage.[[33]](#cite_note-rev-33)

### Human dependency on technology

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Emberverse_series&action=edit&section=25 "Edit section: Human dependency on technology")]

Stirling also explores humanity's dependence on modern technology and to what conditions we might be forced to regress if we were denied our advanced technology. Thomas Wagner, reviewing *Dies the Fire*, said the aftermath of [Hurricane Katrina](/wiki/Hurricane_Katrina "Hurricane Katrina") gave the novel "a degree of gravitas stronger than it would have had otherwise."[[36]](#cite_note-36) Fredric Smoler, writing for *American Heritage*, notes that "reason still allows men and women the (sadly reduced) power to manipulate their environment", but "it is not obvious how a scientific (or feminist) worldview will survive in a newly feudalized, increasingly religious, and fundamentally agrarian world."[[37]](#cite_note-AmHerit-37)

### First world more likely to survive

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Emberverse_series&action=edit&section=26 "Edit section: First world more likely to survive")]

Another theme of the series is that [First World](/wiki/First_World "First World") countries are likely to survive the Change better than [Third World](/wiki/Third_World "Third World") countries. Stirling speculates that the developed world is a larger repository of handicraft lore than the undeveloped world, because the latter group heavily relies on First World castoffs and is too busy on a day-to-day basis to keep up the old ways, while people in the First World have the leisure to become expert horse riders or bow makers.[[33]](#cite_note-rev-33) This idea is originally expressed in-character by people in former First World areas, and later in the series other characters remark that the assumption is unfounded based on exploration many years post-Change.

### Myth and magic

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Emberverse_series&action=edit&section=27 "Edit section: Myth and magic")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | This article needs to be **updated**. Please help update this section to reflect recent events or newly available information. *(October 2012)* |

In the novels, it often appears that magic exists in the world since the Change. When asked whether he was leaving room for magic in the Emberverse, Stirling replied that he could not answer that since he did not wish to give away any plot points, but admitted that by *The Scourge of God*, people will begin to decide whether or not "magic" is possible in the series.[[27]](#cite_note-ph-27) Critic Harriet Klausner described *Scourge* as being like a Greek tragedy due to certain characters receiving visions from "gods", incidents of demonic possessions, cursed arrows, and other uses of magic.[[38]](#cite_note-scific-38) In a dialogue between a mercenary and [Tezcatlipoca](/wiki/Tezcatlipoca "Tezcatlipoca"), the Aztec deity reveals that humanity's lack of attention towards the gods allowed "monsters below the horizon" to bring about the Change.[[39]](#cite_note-39) As the character of Father Ignatius says, however, there is always a rational explanation available: he notes that Ingolf's capture by Kuttner when Kuttner simply spoke a word at the end of *The Sunrise Lands* was probably the result of a [post-hypnotic suggestion](/wiki/Post-hypnotic_suggestion "Post-hypnotic suggestion").[[40]](#cite_note-40)
Reviewer Dale Arnold of the Baltimore Science Fiction Society suggests that magic has real political and societal impact in the Emberverse, whether or not it is actually the result of supernatural force or merely religious delusions, because the characters *act* as if it were real.[[41]](#cite_note-41)

As of *The Sea Peoples* (2017), "magic" is increasingly evident, yet still remains deniable by those of a rationalist mind who do not directly experience it. In particular, approximately half the action of this work takes place in alternate realms which are experienced only through the agency of [shamanic](/wiki/Shamanic "Shamanic") magic.[[42]](#cite_note-42)[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]
